items.teaser: Practical orientation points when old foundations no longer work.

items.title: What anxiety is really saying

items.question: How long does one session take?
items.answer: A regular session takes 50 minutes. The first intro call is 15 minutes and free.

items.question: What is the consultation format?
items.answer: Online via Zoom, Google Meet, or messenger of your choice. Audio or video.

items.question: Do you work in English?
items.answer: Yes, sessions are available in Ukrainian and English. Select your language in the request form.

items.question: How quickly do you reply?
items.answer: I reply to all requests within 24 hours, often on the same day.

items.question: Are sessions confidential?
items.answer: Yes. Everything you share stays between us. Exceptions are described in the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

items.question: Can I cancel a session?
items.answer: Yes, free cancellation is possible up to 24 hours before the session. Later cancellations are charged in full.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy and Code of Ethics
privacy.intro: Trust is the foundation of our work. This document explains how I handle your information and the ethical principles I follow in my practice.

sections.title: 1. Principle of Confidentiality
sections.content: Everything you share during sessions remains strictly between us. I do not disclose your name, the content of our conversations, or the fact that you have sought my services.

sections.title: Exceptions to Confidentiality
sections.content: Pursuant to legislation and professional ethics — a clear risk of harm to yourself (suicidal intent) or to others; reports of the commission of a serious crime; receipt of an official request from law enforcement agencies in accordance with the current legislation of Ukraine; professional supervision — discussing complex cases with expert colleagues — in which all data is strictly anonymized and your name and any identifying details are never revealed.

sections.title: 1.1. Confidentiality in Specific Cases
sections.content:
Features of family therapy: when working with a couple, confidentiality extends to both partners; the psychologist shall not disclose information received from one partner to third parties. The initiating partner (who scheduled the first session) is responsible for ensuring the other partner is aware of this Policy before joint work begins. The psychologist adheres to a "No Secrets" principle within the couple: information shared by one partner in an individual conversation may be brought to a joint discussion if it is necessary to achieve therapeutic goals.

Confidentiality in working with minors: parents or legal guardians are responsible for explaining the essence of this Privacy Policy to the child in an accessible way, considering their age and development. The content of conversations with a minor is confidential. Parents have the right to receive general updates on counseling progress, but specific details of conversations are disclosed to them only with the child's consent or in cases where there is a threat to the child's life or health.

sections.title: 2. Data Collection and Storage
sections.content: I only collect data necessary for communication (name, phone number, e-mail). Your contact details are never transferred to third parties for marketing or advertising purposes. Any notes I may take during a session for professional purposes are stored in a secure location without including your last name.

sections.title: 3. Professional Boundaries
sections.content: I do not enter into friendly, romantic, or business relationships with Clients outside of therapy. If we happen to meet in a public place, I will not greet you first. This is to avoid putting you in an awkward position and to keep the fact of our professional relationship private.

sections.title: 4. The "Do No Harm" Principle
sections.content: I practice only within the scope of my competence. If I feel that your request requires the assistance of another specialist (e.g., a psychiatrist, endocrinologist, or lawyer), I will inform you and provide the appropriate recommendations.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: For data-related requests, contact us at <email>{{email}}</email>.

terms.title: Public Offer Agreement for Psychological Counseling Services
terms.intro: This document constitutes an official and public offer (the "Offer") by psychologist Yaroslav Doroshenko (the "Service Provider") to any individual (the "Client") who accepts the terms of this Agreement.

sections.title: 1. Subject of the Agreement
sections.content:
1.1. The Service Provider undertakes to provide psychological counseling services, and the Client undertakes to accept and pay for them under the terms specified in this Offer.

1.2. Services may be provided either in person or remotely (via video communication).

sections.title: 2. Acceptance Procedure
sections.content:
2.1. Full and unconditional acceptance of this Offer is deemed to occur when the Client makes payment for the first consultation, completes the registration form on the website, or schedules a consultation.

2.2. Upon acceptance, the Agreement is considered concluded in written form.

2.3. Family counseling. In the event of a couple (two partners) booking a consultation, the payment for the service or the physical presence of both partners at the session is considered full and unconditional acceptance of the terms of this Offer by both participants. The partner who makes the booking or payment assumes the obligation to familiarize the other partner with the text of this Offer and the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

2.4. Working with minors. If the recipient of the services is under the age of 18, acceptance of the Offer shall be carried out by their parents or legal guardians. By entering into this Agreement, the parents (guardians) confirm their consent for providing psychological assistance to the child and undertake to facilitate compliance with the terms of the Agreement. The consent form is available on the <parentalConsent>Parental Consent</parentalConsent> page.

sections.title: 3. Rights and Obligations of the Parties
sections.content:
The Service Provider undertakes to: provide professional psychological assistance in accordance with ethical standards; maintain full confidentiality (except in cases provided for by the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>).

The Client undertakes to: attend sessions on time; notify the Service Provider of any cancellation or rescheduling no later than 24 hours prior to the scheduled start time; be sincere and actively work on their request.

sections.title: 4. Cost of Services and Payment Terms
sections.content:
4.1. The cost of consultations is indicated on the website in the pricing section (or communicated when booking the first consultation).

4.2. Payment is made via 100% prepayment or in another manner by prior agreement.

4.3. In the event of a cancellation by the Client less than 24 hours before the session, the fee for the consultation is non-refundable (or remains payable at 100% of the cost).

sections.title: 5. Liability of the Parties
sections.content:
5.1. The psychologist is not liable for how the Client utilizes the information received or for the decisions the Client makes in their personal life.

5.2. Important note: psychological counseling is not medical aid and does not replace treatment by a psychiatrist or other medical doctors.

sections.title: Contacts
sections.content: For organizational questions, contact <email>{{email}}</email> or Telegram <tg>{{telegramHandle}}</tg>.

parentalConsent.title:
Parental Consent
(legal representatives)
parentalConsent.subtitle: For psychological counseling or assessment of a minor
parentalConsent.intro: If the client is under 18, written consent from one parent or legal guardian is required before work begins. Below is a short process overview and a link to download the form.
processSteps: Download the consent PDF form using the link below.
Fill in the form by hand or on a computer and sign it. For teenagers older than 14, adding the child's own signature next to the parent's can support the therapeutic alliance.
If the parents are divorced, it is ideally better to obtain consent from both, or make sure the signing parent has legal authority and the other parent does not object to therapy.
Send a scan or photo of the signed document to <email>{{email}}</email> or via Telegram <tg>{{telegramHandle}}</tg> before the first session.
containsItems: Details of one parent or legal guardian and details of the child.
Confirmation of voluntary consent to psychological services (individual counseling, psychological assessment, corrective sessions, therapeutic process).
Parents' obligation to explain counseling and the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy> to the child in an age-appropriate way.
Information access rules: general progress can be shared with parents, while conversation details are disclosed only with the child's consent or when there is a risk to the child's life or health.
Consent to discuss the case within professional supervision with full anonymization of personal data.
Confirmation of acknowledgment of the <terms>Public Offer</terms> and the <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.
The right to withdraw consent at any time by written or verbal notice.
parentalConsent.questions: If you have questions, please email <email>{{email}}</email> or message in Telegram <tg>{{telegramHandle}}</tg>.
